# apmask_apsum { #astroapers.apmask_apsum }

```python
apmask_apsum(weights, bbox, image, mask=None, *, return_npix=True)
```

Return aperture sum, and npix by default, for raw aperture weights.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**weights**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Bbox-tight aperture weights with shape ``bbox.shape``.

<code>[**bbox**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [BoundingBox]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Location of the bbox-tight weights in ``image`` coordinates.

<code>[**image**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Two-dimensional image to sample.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with the same shape as ``image``. ``True`` pixels contribute zero weight.

<code>[**return_npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If `True`, return ``(apsum, npix)``. If `False`, return only ``apsum``.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**apsum**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of ``image * weight`` over the overlapping, unmasked footprint. Accumulation uses `float64`, even when both the image and stored weights are `float32`.

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Sum of the contributing aperture weights. This can be smaller than the analytic aperture area when the aperture is clipped by the image edge or by ``mask``. Accumulation uses `float64`. Returned only when ``return_npix=True``.
